name: Design System Foundation - Cohesive Premium Design with Enhanced Color System
description: ENHANCED 12-COLOR SEMANTIC SYSTEM (DRIBBBLE ANALYSIS): DARK THEME (PRIMARY) - Deep black-blue base (#02030F not pure black, from Cloutzen), dark navy cards (#0E1558), elevated surfaces (#18181B), subtle borders (#27272A). PRIMARY COLORS - Apex purple (#4926FA unchanged), success green (#17CA29 unchanged, #0E7A1A dark variant). ACCENT COLORS (NEW) - Bright blue (#273ADB from Cloutzen), accent pink (#D82F71 from SaaS Financial Dashboard). ALERT COLORS - Warning yellow (#FFB020), error red (#D4292A). MUTED COLORS (for charts) - Purple-gray (#5E648E), blue-gray (#6D8BAE), neutral gray (#A09DA2). LIGHT THEME - Off-white background (#FAFAFA not pure white), light gray secondary (#EEEEEE). 3-TIER CARD HIERARCHY: Primary cards (GEO Score) = 2px primary border + shadow glow, Secondary cards (Recs) = 1px muted border + subtle shadow, Tertiary cards (lists) = transparent bg + minimal border. GLASSMORPHISM: Modal overlay (rgba(2,3,15,0.8) + blur(20px)), Card glass (rgba(14,21,88,0.6) + blur(12px)), Tooltip (rgba(14,21,88,0.95) + blur(8px)). TYPOGRAPHY - Inter Display (600-700) for headings, Inter (400-500) for body. ANIMATIONS - 150ms standard transitions, 250ms modals, 800ms gauge entrance. CHART COLORS - Primary (#273ADB), secondary (#4926FA), tertiary (#17CA29), categorical array [#2E6DC7, #7586CF, #5E648E, #6D8BAE]. WHITE-LABEL ARCHITECTURE: CSS custom properties from env vars, brand preset system (config/brand-presets.ts), ThemeProvider component, Tailwind var() usage. CRITICAL PRINCIPLE: 'Change colors, keep structure' - maximum 3-4 accent colors, no rainbow UI, consistent card hierarchy prevents piecemeal appearance.
test_description: Design system shows: 1) Dark-first architecture with #02030F deep background, 2) Enhanced 12-color semantic system (primary purple, success green, accent blue, accent pink, warning, error, 3 muted grays), 3) 3-tier card hierarchy (primary/secondary/tertiary with distinct styling), 4) Glassmorphism on modals/overlays only (not main cards), 5) Unified chart color system (4 categorical colors), 6) Inter font family with proper weights, 7) Standardized animations (150ms/250ms/800ms), 8) Maximum 3-4 accent colors per view (no rainbow), 9) Restrained color palette maintains cohesion, 10) CSS variables support white-label switching, 11) Can change NEXT_PUBLIC_BRAND_PRESET to switch brands. Premium cohesive appearance - every component feels like same design system.
design_reference: docs/DRIBBBLE_DESIGN_ANALYSIS.md (NEW - 7 cohesive patterns), docs/VISUAL_DESIGN_RESEARCH.md (Dribbble Research), docs/BRAND_VALUES_AND_POSITIONING.md (White-Label Architecture), docs/WHITE_LABEL_ARCHITECTURE.md (Technical implementation)
dribbble_inspiration: Wallet Dashboard (white mode) - #FAFAFB off-white, #484FB5 blue-purple
SaaS Financial Dashboard (dark) - #040405 near-black, #D82F71 pink accent
Marketing Automation Platform - #2E6DC7 blue, #6D8BAE blue-gray
Cloutzen AI Landing - #02030F deep black-blue, #273ADB bright blue

white_label_requirements.tailwind_config_updates: All colors must use var(--primary), var(--secondary), etc. All fonts must use var(--font-heading), var(--font-body). Border radius from var(--border-radius). Spacing from calc(var(--spacing-scale) * N).

validation_rules: Implement 12-color semantic system with exact hex values: primary (#4926FA), success (#17CA29), accent-blue (#273ADB), accent-pink (#D82F71), warning (#FFB020), error (#D4292A), background-dark (#02030F), card-dark (#0E1558), and 3 muted grays
Enforce 3-tier card hierarchy with specific styling: Primary (2px border + shadow glow), Secondary (1px border + subtle shadow), Tertiary (transparent + minimal border)
Apply glassmorphism ONLY to modals/overlays (rgba blur effects), NEVER to main content cards
Use Inter font family exclusively: Inter Display (600-700 weight) for headings, Inter (400-500 weight) for body text
Implement standardized animation timing: 150ms for standard transitions, 250ms for modals, 800ms for gauge entrance animations
Limit to maximum 3-4 accent colors per view to prevent 'rainbow UI' anti-pattern
All colors must be defined as CSS custom properties (--color-primary, --color-success, etc.) sourced from environment variables
Support white-label brand switching via NEXT_PUBLIC_BRAND_PRESET environment variable
Use Tailwind var() syntax for all color references: bg-[var(--color-primary)]
TypeScript strict mode - zero 'any' types, export all interfaces for theme types

code_examples.title: Global CSS Variables (app/globals.css)
code_examples.explanation: Defines the complete 12-color semantic system as CSS custom properties. These variables can be overridden based on NEXT_PUBLIC_BRAND_PRESET for white-label support. All components must reference these variables instead of hardcoded colors.

code_examples.title: 3-Tier Card Hierarchy Components (components/ui/cards.tsx)
code_examples.explanation: Implements the 3-tier card hierarchy with distinct visual hierarchy: Primary cards for critical content (GEO Score), Secondary for important content (Recommendations), Tertiary for lists. Each tier has specific border width, shadow, and opacity to create visual depth without clutter.

code_examples.title: Glassmorphism for Modals Only (components/ui/modal-overlay.tsx)
code_examples.explanation: Demonstrates proper glassmorphism usage: ONLY for modal overlays and floating elements. Uses rgba with backdrop-blur to create depth. Main content cards NEVER use glassmorphism - they use solid backgrounds for readability. Animation duration matches 250ms modal standard.

code_examples.title: Brand Preset System (config/brand-presets.ts)
code_examples.explanation: White-label brand preset system that allows switching entire brand identities via NEXT_PUBLIC_BRAND_PRESET. Each preset defines complete color palette, fonts, and logos. The design structure (card hierarchy, animations, layout) remains constant - only colors/branding changes. This ensures consistency while allowing customization.

common_pitfalls: ÃƒÆ’Ã‚Â¢ÃƒÂ¯Ã‚Â¿Ã‚Â½Ãƒâ€¦Ã¢â‚¬â„¢ Using pure black (#000000) for dark backgrounds - MUST use deep black-blue (#02030F) for premium feel
ÃƒÆ’Ã‚Â¢ÃƒÂ¯Ã‚Â¿Ã‚Â½Ãƒâ€¦Ã¢â‚¬â„¢ Applying glassmorphism to main content cards - Glassmorphism is ONLY for modals/overlays/tooltips
ÃƒÆ’Ã‚Â¢ÃƒÂ¯Ã‚Â¿Ã‚Â½Ãƒâ€¦Ã¢â‚¬â„¢ Using more than 3-4 accent colors per view - Creates 'rainbow UI' and destroys cohesion
ÃƒÆ’Ã‚Â¢ÃƒÂ¯Ã‚Â¿Ã‚Â½Ãƒâ€¦Ã¢â‚¬â„¢ Hardcoding color values instead of CSS variables - Breaks white-label functionality
ÃƒÆ’Ã‚Â¢ÃƒÂ¯Ã‚Â¿Ã‚Â½Ãƒâ€¦Ã¢â‚¬â„¢ Making all cards look identical - MUST implement 3-tier hierarchy (primary/secondary/tertiary)
ÃƒÆ’Ã‚Â¢ÃƒÂ¯Ã‚Â¿Ã‚Â½Ãƒâ€¦Ã¢â‚¬â„¢ Using wrong font weights - Inter Display must be 600-700 for headings, Inter 400-500 for body
ÃƒÆ’Ã‚Â¢ÃƒÂ¯Ã‚Â¿Ã‚Â½Ãƒâ€¦Ã¢â‚¬â„¢ Inconsistent animation timing - MUST use 150ms/250ms/800ms standards, not random durations
ÃƒÆ’Ã‚Â¢ÃƒÂ¯Ã‚Â¿Ã‚Â½Ãƒâ€¦Ã¢â‚¬â„¢ Creating new colors on-the-fly - Stick to the 12-color semantic system exclusively
ÃƒÆ’Ã‚Â¢ÃƒÂ¯Ã‚Â¿Ã‚Â½Ãƒâ€¦Ã¢â‚¬â„¢ Using inline styles or CSS modules - MUST use Tailwind utility classes with var() syntax
ÃƒÆ’Ã‚Â¢ÃƒÂ¯Ã‚Â¿Ã‚Â½Ãƒâ€¦Ã¢â‚¬â„¢ Forgetting TypeScript types for theme configuration - All brand preset interfaces must be exported
implementation_guidance: Start with globals.css: Define all 12 CSS custom properties first, ensure they can be overridden by brand presets loaded from environment variables
Implement card hierarchy early: Create PrimaryCard, SecondaryCard, TertiaryCard components first - all other components will compose these
Test white-label switching: Create at least 2 brand presets (Apex + Enterprise) and verify NEXT_PUBLIC_BRAND_PRESET environment variable correctly updates all colors
Use design tokens consistently: Never hardcode colors - always reference CSS variables via Tailwind's var() syntax to maintain white-label flexibility
Limit accent color usage: Each view should use maximum 3-4 colors (primary + 2-3 accents). Use muted grays for secondary information. This creates cohesive, premium appearance instead of 'rainbow UI'
Validate glassmorphism usage: Grep for 'backdrop-blur' in codebase - should ONLY appear in modal/overlay/tooltip components, NEVER in main content cards
Typography hierarchy enforcement: Use Inter Display (600-700) exclusively for headings (h1-h6), Inter (400-500) for all body text, links, and labels. Never mix font families.

name: Dashboard Shell UI - Linear-Style Premium Layout
description: Main dashboard layout with Linear-inspired design: HEADER - Height 56px, bg #18181B, brand logo left, global search (Ctrl+K hint), notification bell, user avatar with dropdown. SIDEBAR - Width 240px (56px collapsed), bg slightly darker than content (#0F0F10), Lucide icons 20px, active item has primary color left border + subtle bg highlight, smooth collapse animation. CONTENT AREA - bg #0A0A0B, max-width container, consistent 24px padding. No rainbow colors - neutral grays with single accent color.
test_description: Dashboard shell shows: 1) Dark header with proper elevation, 2) Sidebar with icon-only collapse mode, 3) Active nav item has blue left border highlight, 4) Smooth 150ms sidebar collapse animation, 5) Consistent 20px Lucide icons, 6) Neutral color palette (no bright colors except accent). Matches Linear/Vercel aesthetic.

name: Sidebar Navigation UI - Premium Design
description: Collapsible sidebar matching wireframes: Dashboard, Recs (with count badge), Score, Integrations, Settings. Shows recommendation counts by priority (High/Med/Low). Quick Action button at bottom. DRIBBBLE PATTERN: Outcrowd-style pill-shaped active state highlighting instead of left border. Ronas IT-style vertical nav icons with tooltip labels on collapse. Fireart Studio-style subtle hover states with slight background tint.
test_description: Sidebar shows navigation with Recs badge showing count, priority breakdown (High 3, Med 5, Low 12). Collapses on mobile with hamburger menu. Professional styling with proper active states.
dribbble_inspiration: Outcrowd Boostboard - pill-shaped active states
Ronas IT - vertical nav with tooltip labels
Fireart Studio - subtle hover states

name: Dark/Light Theme Toggle
description: Theme switcher with system preference detection. Colors follow UI_UX_DESIGN_STRATEGY.md color system.
test_description: Toggle switches theme and persists choice to localStorage. Dark theme uses proper dark colors, not just inverted.

name: Dashboard Home Page UI - Premium Design
description: Dashboard home matching wireframes: Large central GEO Score gauge with circular display (score 72, grade B+, trend +12 points). Three sub-score progress bars (Schema Quality 85, Content Completeness 68, AI Visibility 74). High Priority Recommendations section with action buttons. Recent Activity feed. Quick Stats cards. DRIBBBLE PATTERN: Conceptzilla ERP-style KPI cards with large numeric values (32px+), small muted labels, green/red trend indicators. Outcrowd-style data visualization with consistent spacing. Monkyne keyword analytics-style layout with main metric prominent, secondary metrics in row below.
test_description: Dashboard shows: 1) Large circular GEO Score gauge with letter grade, 2) Three sub-score cards with progress bars, 3) High Priority Recs with red/amber priority badges and Implement/Schedule/View buttons, 4) Recent Activity timeline with relative timestamps, 5) Quick Stats grid. Professional enterprise appearance.
dribbble_inspiration: Conceptzilla ERP - KPI cards with large metrics, small labels
Outcrowd Boostboard - consistent data visualization
Monkyne Analytics - main metric prominent with secondaries below

name: GEO Score Gauge Component - Premium Circular SVG
description: Linear-inspired circular gauge: SVG circle with gradient arc stroke (primary blue gradient), score number in center (Inter Display 48px bold), letter grade below (24px muted), subtle glow/shadow on arc, smooth entrance animation (0 to score over 800ms with easeOutQuart). Trend indicator with arrow icon and delta value. Arc uses strokeLinecap round, strokeWidth 8-12px. Background track in muted gray (#27272A). DRIBBBLE PATTERN: Monkyne-style circular score display (90.2% pattern) with clean percentage in center, keyword/label below, bright green (#17CA29) for high scores, amber for medium, red for low. Green accent color for positive metrics.
test_description: GEOScoreGauge shows: 1) SVG circular gauge with gradient blue arc, 2) Large centered score (48px Inter Display), 3) Letter grade in muted color below, 4) Smooth 800ms entrance animation, 5) Subtle shadow/glow on arc, 6) Muted gray background track. Matches premium analytics dashboard aesthetic (Stripe/Linear style).
design_reference: docs/VISUAL_DESIGN_RESEARCH.md - GEO Score Gauge section, docs/UI_WIREFRAMES.md
dribbble_inspiration: Monkyne Analytics - circular 90.2% gauge with centered percentage
Conceptzilla ERP - green accent (#17CA29) for positive metrics

name: Monitor - Platform Selection UI - Premium Cards
description: Toggle cards to select AI platforms (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity, Grok, DeepSeek) with each platform having its own branded color accent, icon, and connection status indicator. Cards show last scan time and mention count.
test_description: Platform selection cards with: 1) Platform-specific brand colors and icons, 2) Toggle switch with visual feedback, 3) Connection status indicator, 4) Last scan timestamp, 5) Mention count badge. Professional enterprise appearance.

Sentiment badges use restrained colors (green #17CA29 positive, gray #8A878A neutral, red #FB1306 negative). Outcrowd-style platform icons with subtle colored backgrounds.

Monkyne-style horizontal filter row with clear spacing. Conceptzilla ERP-style dropdown selects with subtle borders. Filter chips use muted colors (#E4E4E7 inactive, #4926FA active).

name: Monitor - Search Prompt Performance View - Premium Design
description: Track which user search queries trigger brand mentions across AI platforms (inspired by Otterly). Shows prompt text, platforms where brand appeared, frequency, sentiment per prompt. Sortable table with search, export capability. Helps users understand which queries drive AI visibility.
test_description: Search Prompt Performance view shows: 1) Table of search prompts with brand mention results, 2) Platform icons showing where brand appeared, 3) Frequency count and trend, 4) Sentiment indicator per prompt, 5) Search/filter by prompt text, 6) Export to CSV. Professional enterprise appearance.

name: Recommendations - Dashboard UI - Linear-Style Cards
description: Linear-inspired recommendation cards: CARD - bg #18181B, 12px radius, subtle border (#27272A), hover: slight elevation increase. PRIORITY BADGE - Small pill, muted colors (critical #DC2626/10% opacity bg, high #EA580C, medium #D97706, low #65A30D). TYPE BADGE - Outlined pill with gray border. METRICS - Impact/Effort/Confidence as subtle inline text or mini progress bars, NOT bright colored pills. ACTIONS - Ghost buttons by default, primary CTA solid blue. EXPAND - Smooth accordion animation. DRIBBBLE PATTERN: Conceptzilla ERP-style clean cards with off-white (#FAF9FA) background in light mode, muted text (#8A878A), green success accents (#17CA29). Outcrowd-style pill-shaped tabs for filtering by status/priority.
test_description: Recommendations page shows: 1) Cards with subtle dark backgrounds (#18181B), 2) Priority badges using muted semantic colors on transparent bg, 3) Metrics displayed subtly (not garish), 4) Ghost action buttons with hover states, 5) Smooth expand/collapse animation (200ms), 6) Consistent with Linear/Notion card aesthetic. No bright multicolor appearance.
dribbble_inspiration: Conceptzilla ERP - clean cards with off-white bg, muted text, green accents
Outcrowd Boostboard - pill-shaped filter tabs

Chart container with card styling matching dashboard. DRIBBBLE PATTERN: Ronas IT-style area charts with soft gradient fills (#4926FA to transparent), subtle grid lines, clean axis labels. Fireart Studio-style smooth curves with hover crosshairs. Outcrowd-style legend placement below chart.
test_description: Chart shows: 1) Smooth line with gradient area fill, 2) Proper date axis formatting, 3) Hover tooltips with mention details, 4) Card container matching dashboard aesthetic, 5) Responsive on mobile. Professional Stripe/Linear appearance.

Thick donut stroke with rounded ends.

name: Crisis Alert System - Threshold-Based Detection
description: Automated crisis detection inspired by Brand24: Configurable thresholds for sentiment drops (e.g., >20% negative in 24hrs), mention spikes, or score drops. Triggers immediate multi-channel alerts (email, Slack, WhatsApp, in-app). Crisis dashboard showing timeline of events, severity indicator, and suggested response actions.
test_description: Crisis Alert system shows: 1) Settings page to configure thresholds (sentiment %, mention spike %, score drop), 2) Multi-channel alert triggers when threshold exceeded, 3) Crisis dashboard with event timeline, 4) Severity indicator (Warning/Critical/Emergency), 5) Suggested response actions. Prevents crises from spreading undetected.
design_reference: Brand24 - real-time crisis alerts and detection pattern

name: Phase 7.0 - Cheerio Crawling Enhancement
description: Add Cheerio-based DOM extraction layer for team and social discovery from web pages
test_description: createExtractor(html) returns working extraction methods, extractTeamFromHtml finds team members, extractSocialsFromHtml finds social links

name: Phase 7.1 - Social Media Database Schema
description: Create social_accounts, social_mentions, social_metrics, social_scores tables
test_description: drizzle-kit push succeeds, all tables queryable via db.select()

name: Phase 7.2 - People/Leadership Database Schema
description: Create brand_people, people_ai_mentions, people_scores tables with role categories
test_description: Can insert/query people with role_category enum (c_suite, founder, board, key_employee, ambassador)

name: Phase 7.3 - SMO Scoring Algorithm
description: Social Media Optimization score with 5 sub-components (reach 25%, engagement 30%, sentiment 20%, growth 15%, consistency 10%)
test_description: calculateSMOScore returns score 0-100 with breakdown, logarithmic follower scaling works correctly

name: Phase 7.3 - PPO Scoring Algorithm
description: People Presence Optimization score with 4 sub-components (executive visibility 35%, thought leadership 30%, AI mentions 20%, social engagement 15%)
test_description: calculatePPOScore returns score 0-100 with breakdown, role weighting works correctly

name: Phase 7.3 - Unified 5-Component Score
description: Update unified score to 5 components: SEO 25%, GEO 25%, AEO 15%, SMO 20%, PPO 15% with legacy 3-component fallback
test_description: calculateUnifiedScore returns full 5-component score when SMO/PPO data available, falls back to 3-component legacy mode otherwise

name: Phase 7.4 - Social Dashboard UI
description: Social media dashboard with SMO gauge, connected accounts, sentiment analysis, recent mentions, recommendations
test_description: Social page shows SMO score gauge with breakdown bars, stat cards, sentiment chart, empty states for no data

name: Phase 7.5 - People Dashboard UI
description: People presence dashboard with PPO gauge, key people list, AI mentions, thought leadership metrics, recommendations
test_description: People page shows PPO score gauge with breakdown bars, person cards with role badges, AI mention cards, empty states

name: Phase 7 - Social API Routes
description: API routes for social summary, accounts, mentions, metrics at /api/social with type query param
test_description: GET /api/social?brandId=X&type=summary returns SMO score and breakdown, type=accounts returns connected accounts

name: Phase 7 - People API Routes
description: API routes for people summary, list, AI mentions at /api/people with type query param, POST to add person
test_description: GET /api/people?brandId=X&type=summary returns PPO score, type=list returns people array, POST creates new person
